results.title: The Tenderness of wolves = Die Zartlichkeit der Wolfe
results.creator_credit: Ulli Lommel, Rainer Werner Fassbinder and Tango Film Production Number Four
results.brief_description: Based on the life and crimes of Felix Haarmann, the German child murderer who’s killing spree in Dusseldorf during the depression was also the subject of Fritz Lang’s M (1931), and a later version directed by Joseph Losey (1951). Not surprising then that lead actor Kurt Raab, (who also wrote the screenplay and served as art director) pays homage to Peter Lorre in his portrayal of the paedophilic child murderer and sometimes police informer. Produced by Rainer Werner Fassbinder’s production company, (he also makes a brief appearence) ‘The Tenderness of Wolves’, although directed by Ulli Lommel, feels and looks much more like a Fassbinder film and in fact was made during an extended break from “Effie Briest” (1974), which also features Kurt Raab. Ulli Lommel updates the film to post World War Two (mainly due to budget restrictions), and presents the killer as a resourceful, angst- ridden man, who after vampirising the boys would sell their dismembered bodies and clothes on the black market. It’s in Haarmann’s many social connections and the complicity of his friends and police (the tender wolves of the title), that the film draws some of it’s more darker moments. Ultimately, a critique about the society that this monster is allowed to flourish in, ‘The Tenderness of Wolves’ is a disturbing and unsettling experience. In German with English subtitles.

results.title: French cancan
results.creator_credit: Jean Renoir, Louis Wipf and Franco London Film (Paris)
results.brief_description: Beautifully photographed, this comedy-drama chronicles the revival of Paris’ most notorious dance as it tells the story of a theater producer who turns a humble washerwoman into a star at the Moulin Rouge. Renoir’s return to film-making in France after 15 years absence is an exuberant, affectionate homage to the theatre of ‘La Belle Epoque’ (Edith Piaf and Tatachou appear briefly as stars of the day.) It also gave him the opportunity to work with Gabin again, having made three films with him in the 1930s. Cast includes Jean Gabin, Francoise Arnoul and Maria Felix. In French with English subtitles.

results.title: The Silence of the lambs [Portuguese subtitles]
results.creator_credit: Jonathan Demme, Edward Saxon, Kenneth Utt, Ron Bozman, Orion Pictures Corporation and Strongheart Films
results.brief_description: Based on the best-selling novel by Thomas Harris, “Silence of the Lambs” is a disturbing and chilling thriller. The film which won five Academy Awards, is highlighted by the superb performances of Jodie Foster as FBI cadet Clarice Starling and Anthony Hopkins as the psychotic criminal, once brilliant psychiatrist, Dr. Hannibal “the Cannibal” Lecter. Starling’s job is to tap Lector’s mind in order to find the serial killer known as “Buffalo Bill”, a killer who skins his victims. Lector uses his macabre knowledge to entice Clarice into intricate but chilling mindgames. The film’s focal point is not so much the catching of the killer, but the relationship between Starling and Lector. The audience is manipulated so that Lecter is simultaneously repellent and charismatic, thus contributing to the tension and unease of the film. In English with Portugese subtitles.

results.title: Dances with wolves [Portuguese subtitles]
results.creator_credit: Kevin Costner, Jim Wilson, Majestic Films International and TIG Productions
results.brief_description: Assigned to an abandoned army post, Civil War hero John Dunbar (Kevin Costner), finds himself alone and beyond civilisation. Only a wolf and some roving Sioux Indians provide distractions. Curiosity and survival force Dunbar to overcome his fear and he develops a unique relationship with the Sioux. But as the US military ruthlessly pushes west, the Indians’ existence is threatened and Dunbar finds himself torn between two cultures. Honoured with seven Academy Awards including Best Picture (1991), this epic is a cinematic masterpiece of scope, beauty, spirituality and rousing adventure. Dean Semler’s photography is breathtaking. Screenplay by Michael Blake, based on his novel. In English with Portugese subtitles.

results.title: The Crime of Mr Lange = Le Crime de Monsieur Lange
results.creator_credit: Jean Renoir, Andre Halley des Fontaines and Oberon
results.brief_description: Lange is an author of wild west novels. When the owner of the company that publishes Lange’s works absconds with the company funds, Lange rallies the employees together to create their own publishing house. The publisher returns, disguised as a priest, and demands a share of the profits. Lange responds by killing him. The grateful employees help Lange to escape prosecution, allowing him to leave the country with his lovely fiancee. It is a joyous film, just as the Popular Front was a joyous movement, happy in the belief that living and working conditions could be changed for the better and fascism resisted at home and abroad. Exploitation (including sexual exploitation) are represented as the product of authoritarian capitalism, represented here by the publisher. Cast includes Rene Lefevre, Jules Berry, Florelle and Nadia Sibirkskaia. In French with English subtitles.

results.title: It happened here
results.creator_credit: Andrew Mollo, Kevin Brownlow and United Artists Corporation
results.brief_description: This is an imaginative realisation of a German occupation of Britain in July, 1940: Pauline Murray, an Irish girl working in England, joins up in 1944 with the re-emerging Resistance. On a 7,000 pound budget the filmmakers’ recreated a newsreel style but did not use stock or newsreel footage, a direct reaction to fifties war movies which they felt never dealt with the nature of Nazism. A controversial sequence showed real British Nazis expressing their beliefs. The documentary style drama took eight years to complete.

results.title: The Marriage of Maria Braun = die ehe der Maria Braun
results.creator_credit: Rainer Werner Fassbinder, Michael Fengler, Albatros, Filmverlag der Autoren, Trio and WDR
results.brief_description: A tragicomedy spanning a decade which begins with the wedding of Maria Braun amid the chaos of an Allied bomb attack in 1943. Within 24 hours Maria’s husband is sent to the Russian front. Initially naive and idealistic, Maria increasingly adjusts to male values using her sexuality resourcefully but cynically in the pursuit of wealth and power. Yet she retains a romantic yearning for reunion with her missing husband which prevents her from fully living in the present. Fassbinder clearly means the film to be seen as an allegory about the German ‘economic miracle’ - the deep-seated continuity between past and present. Schygulla’s performance is deliberately dead-pan, taking the melodramatic mode to its limits, and reflecting Fassbinder’s interest in the melodramas of Douglas Sirk. In German with English subtitles.

results.title: Bullitt
results.creator_credit: Peter Yates, Philip D'Antoni and Solar Productions
results.brief_description: Steve McQueen plays the title role as Frank Bullitt, a no-glitter, all-guts cop who is assigned to protect Chicago gangster Johnny Ross (Pat Renella), who is scheduled to testify against his mob cronies in San Francisco. But after Ross is attacked and later killed at the hospital, Bullitt conceals Ross’s death and begins to investigate the case himself, sensing something suspicious. One of the most memorable scenes in Bullitt is the celebrated car chase through the streets of San Francisco. McQueen, an expert automobile and motorcycle racer, does his own stunt driving as he propels his high-performance Mustang around and over San Francisco’s fabled hills. Cast includes Jacqueline Bisset, Robert Vaughn, Don Gordon and Robert Duvall.

results.title: From dusk till dawn
results.creator_credit: Robert Rodriguez, Gianni Nunnari, Meir Teper and A Band Apart
results.brief_description: After pulling off a bloody Texas bank heist, notorious bank robber Seth Gecko and his homicidal, rape-happy brother Ritchie, secure a safe passage across the border into Mexico thanks to the unwilling aid of disillusioned ex-minister Jacob Fuller. After kidnapping the Fuller family, the Gecko brothers elude the authorities and reach a supposed safe haven at a pre-chosen site: a surreal and ultra-sleazy bar in the middle of the desert, where they plan to split the loot with their Mexican connection. Unfortunately, the club is actually a haven for ferocious Aztec vampires; a twist which spins the schizophrenic plot into its second phase. Cast includes George Clooney, Quentin Tarantino, Harvey Keitel and Juliette Lewis.

results.title: The Italian job
results.creator_credit: Peter Collinson, Michael Deeley and Paramount Pictures
results.brief_description: When Charlie (Michael Caine) leaves jail he has the opportunity for a better future, or a sure fire way of landing back in jail when he inherits the plans to a $4,000,000 gold robbery. Things look positive when Charlie receives inside help from prison-based Mr Bridger (Noel Coward), who is the mastermind behind the plan. Central to the plan’s success is staging the world’s biggest traffic jam as a diversion to their shady activities. Cast includes Benny Hill, Raf Vallone and Tony Beckley. Music by Quincy Jones.
